expected_findings.summary: 1 RSU vest lot(s) with basis near $0 — likely double-counted
expected_findings.detail: The W-2 has a Box 12 code V (RSU / NQSO income) and the 1099-B has same-day sale-to-cover lots with a cost basis near zero. RSU vest income is already included in W-2 Box 1 wages; the 1099-B lot cost basis should equal the Box 1 inclusion amount, not zero. Leaving the basis at zero double-counts the vest as both wage income and a capital gain. This is the single most common mechanically detectable error in the public TurboTax record — a $3,200 refund delta is in Intuit's own public record.

expected_findings.summary: Passive K-1 loss from Synthetic Partners 41 LP must be suspended
expected_findings.detail: The K-1 is marked passive and has a loss. Per IRC §469 passive losses can only offset passive income. If there is no passive income to absorb the loss this year, it must be suspended on Form 8582 and carried forward.

expected_findings.summary: Consider real-estate-professional status on this rental
expected_findings.detail: The rental is in service for most of the year. If the taxpayer or spouse spent more than 750 hours on real property trades or businesses and materially participated, rental losses become nonpassive and are not subject to §469 limits.

expected_findings.summary: Land not excluded from depreciable basis on Rental City rental
expected_findings.detail: IRS Publication 527 requires residential rental depreciation to be computed on the building value only; land is never depreciable. This property's depreciation record has no land_value / building_value split, so the full purchase price is being depreciated. Using a conservative 25% land allocation, the current year over-deduction is about $2,591. Pull Example County assessor records for the actual split before filing.
Foreign tax paid exceeds the election threshold for claiming the credit without Form 1116. Form 1116 is required and the credit may be limited.

expected_findings.summary: PTET election can effectively uncap the SALT deduction on K-1 income
expected_findings.detail: IRS Notice 2020-75 blessed state-level PTET regimes as a way to pay state tax at the entity level, converting a capped SALT deduction into a federal ordinary business expense that passes through as lower K-1 income. Consider the PTET election with each state where the K-1 generates income.

A prior year state refund is only taxable to the extent it produced a federal tax benefit (the §111 tax benefit rule). If prior year was at the SALT cap, the refund is usually NOT taxable. Do the worksheet.

expected_findings.summary: Morgan has up to $7,000 of TY2025 IRA headroom
expected_findings.detail: Morgan is 39 years old; the TY2025 IRA contribution limit is $7,000. Deductibility may phase out at high AGI for traditional IRA if covered by an employer plan; Roth IRA phases out at $236K–$246K MFJ.
Morgan has contributed $14,000 toward the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Another $9,500 can still be deferred before year end.

expected_findings.summary: Illinois PTET election available on pass-through income
expected_findings.detail: Illinois allows a pass-through entity to elect to pay state tax at the entity level, producing a federal ordinary business deduction that effectively uncaps the SALT cost on this K-1. The return has eligible pass-through income but the state slice is not flagged as having made the election.

expected_findings.summary: MACRS depreciation timing differences can create AMT adjustments
expected_findings.detail: Residential rental (27.5-year) and commercial (39-year) MACRS use straight-line which is identical for regular and AMT, so no adjustment — but personal property (5/7-year) uses 200% DB for regular and 150% DB for AMT. Check any §179 / personal-property depreciation for an addback.

expected_findings.summary: Need $14,645 withheld to hit 90% current year safe harbor
expected_findings.detail: The alternative safe harbor is 90% of current year tax. This is usually only easier to hit than 110% prior year if income dropped.

Based on a shortfall of $2,760 and the TY2025 applicable interest rate of ~8%, the underpayment penalty is approximately $110. Consider the annualized income method (Schedule AI) if income was lumpy across quarters.

expected_findings.summary: Use Schedule AI to reduce §6654 penalty on lumpy income
expected_findings.detail: Income arrived unevenly (RSU vest, K-1 distribution, or both). Form 2210 Schedule AI lets you annualize quarterly income so early quarters with little income do not generate a penalty.
